scope: Validate the first enterprise-response promotion candidate by rerunning it in a benchmark-review lane

decision_context.prior_state: The promotion brief recommended ai-security-questionnaire-copilot for human benchmark review, but that read still came from a run where the candidate received discovery-lane breakout support.
decision_context.decision: Add a benchmark-review path to the hybrid spec and rerun the focused enterprise panel with ai-security-questionnaire-copilot treated as a review candidate rather than a discovered candidate.
decision_context.why_now: Benchmark membership should not be decided from a discovery-favored run.

files_expected.purpose: Allows selected discovered candidates to move into the benchmark-review lane.
Exposes promotion-review movement through the hybrid spec CLI.
Documents promote-domains behavior and the no-breakout-favoritism rule.
Stores the benchmark-review spec for the enterprise panel.
Summarizes whether the nominated candidate still holds up without discovery-lane breakout support.
Captures the benchmark-review validation request and verification plan.

planned_steps: Allow promoted candidates to enter a benchmark-review lane during hybrid spec generation
Compare the result to the earlier focused promotion read
Save an operator note and update tranche logs

expected_checks: The promoted candidate is listed under promotion_review_domain_ids
The breakout shock no longer targets the promoted candidate
The note states whether the candidate still clears maintained benchmark admission

guardrails: Do not auto-admit the review candidate into maintained benchmarks
Keep the validation run in exploratory_frontier
Make the discovery-bias removal explicit in the saved note
